articles.title: CRM URL Down — Odoo Container Recovery

sections.title: Detection Pattern
sections.content: Blackbox Exporter probes http://odoo:8069/web/health every 15 seconds. CRMURLDown fires immediately (0m pending) when probe_success=0. Alert Correlator checks active_alerts — no suppression if CRMURLDown is not already firing. Zammad ticket created automatically.

sections.title: Root Cause
sections.content: Odoo container stopped — external docker stop command, OOM kill, or host-level issue. Health endpoint unreachable — all CRM users blocked. Risk score: 18 — fully autonomous. RCA Engine confirms via Bridge API crm_health=0.

sections.title: Autonomous Remediation
sections.content: Remediation Agent executes: docker compose start odoo. Polls Bridge API every 5 seconds — waits for crm_health=1. Grafana annotation posted with resolution timestamp. Zammad ticket closed with MTTR. Typical MTTR: 52 seconds. No human involvement required.

sections.title: Prevention
sections.content: Ensure Odoo container has restart policy: unless-stopped in docker-compose.yml. Monitor odoo-postgres health — Odoo will not start without database. Set Blackbox Exporter alert threshold at 0m pending for immediate detection.

articles.title: CRM Slow Response — Graceful Restart Under Memory Pressure

sections.title: Detection Pattern
sections.content: Blackbox Exporter latency > 2 seconds for 5 consecutive minutes triggers CRMSlowResponse (5m pending). CRMSlowResponse is suppressed if CRMURLDown is already in active_alerts — avoids duplicate incidents. Risk score: 42 — HiTL required.

sections.title: Root Cause
sections.content: Odoo worker pool saturation under sustained load — memory pressure at 85-95%, long-poll workers exhausted, requests queuing. Typically seen after extended uptime or high concurrent user load. Not a container stop — service is running but degraded.

sections.title: HiTL Remediation
sections.content: RCA Engine sends Slack notification with full diagnostic context — latency, memory, worker saturation metrics. Engineer approves graceful restart via Slack one-click Approve. Remediation Agent executes: docker compose restart odoo. Active sessions terminated gracefully. Typical approval time: 11 seconds. Total MTTR: 3-4 minutes.

sections.title: Grafana Alert Configuration
sections.content: CRMSlowResponse pending period must be 5 minutes — prevents firing before CRMURLDown if container is fully down. CRMURLDown pending period: 0 minutes — fires immediately. This ordering ensures URL down is always handled autonomously before slow response triggers HiTL.

articles.title: Order Queue Spike — Worker Down Recovery

sections.title: Detection Pattern
sections.content: Prometheus scrapes Redis queue_depth via Redis Exporter every 15 seconds. HighQueueDepth alert fires when queue_depth > 50 — indicating demo-worker has stopped consuming. Bridge API reflects queue_depth in real time. Risk score: 12 — fully autonomous.

sections.title: Root Cause
sections.content: demo-worker container stopped — docker stop demo-worker command or container crash. Redis queue is persistent — all queued orders survive the worker restart. No data loss. Queue accumulates at rate of incoming orders until worker restarts.

sections.title: Autonomous Remediation
sections.content: Remediation Agent executes: docker compose start demo-worker. Worker connects to Redis and begins processing queued orders immediately. Bridge API queue_depth drops to 0 as backlog clears. Zammad ticket closed with MTTR. Typical MTTR: 38 seconds. No human involvement required.

sections.title: Verification
sections.content: Monitor Bridge API queue_depth after worker restart — should reach 0 within 2-3 minutes depending on backlog size. If queue_depth remains elevated after 5 minutes, check Redis connectivity and demo-api order generation rate. Prometheus graph shows queue_depth timeline.

articles.title: High Error Rate — demo-api Chaos Mode

sections.title: Detection Pattern
sections.content: Prometheus monitors error_rate from demo-api. HighErrorRate alert fires when error_rate > 0.1 (10%) for sustained period. Risk score: 45 — HiTL required. Triggered manually via chaos endpoint: POST /chaos with body {high_error: true}.

sections.title: Root Cause
sections.content: Chaos mode enabled on demo-api — intentional error injection for demo purposes. In production equivalent: application bug, database connectivity issue, or dependency failure causing high error rates. api_health remains 1 — container is up but responses are failing.

sections.title: HiTL Remediation
sections.content: RCA Engine sends Slack notification. Engineer approves via Slack one-click. Remediation Agent calls chaos endpoint: POST /chaos with body {high_error: false} to disable chaos mode. Error rate returns to 0 within one Prometheus scrape interval (15 seconds). Zammad ticket closed.

sections.title: Chaos Endpoints
sections.content: Enable high error rate: POST http://localhost:8080/chaos {high_error: true}. Enable slow database: POST http://localhost:8080/chaos {slow_db: true}. Restore all: POST http://localhost:8080/chaos {high_error: false, slow_db: false}. Use for demo scenario 3 — HiTL Guardian demonstration.

articles.title: ZeroOps RCA Service — Risk Thresholds and Runbook

sections.title: Risk Score Thresholds
sections.content: Autonomous (Sentinel): risk < 25 — executes without human gate. Applies to: CRMURLDown (18), HighQueueDepth (12), ServiceURLDown (18). HiTL (Guardian): risk 25-74 — Slack approval required. Applies to: CRMSlowResponse (42), HighErrorRate (45), HighLatency (38), SlowDatabase (38). Escalate: risk > 74 — on-call engineer paged directly.

sections.title: Suppression Rules
sections.content: CRMSlowResponse suppressed when CRMURLDown is in active_alerts — prevents HiTL firing when container is fully down. ServiceSlowResponse suppressed when ServiceURLDown is active. Suppression checked on every incoming alert. active_alerts dict maintained in rca-service memory — cleared when alert resolves in Grafana.

sections.title: Grafana Alert Configuration
sections.content: CRMURLDown: 0m pending — fires immediately on probe failure. CRMSlowResponse: 5m pending — fires after 5 minutes of latency > 2s. HighQueueDepth: configured in Prometheus rules. All alerts route to Alertmanager webhook at http://rca-service:5000/webhook. Grafana annotations posted by Change Validator after every action.

sections.title: Stack Recovery
sections.content: If RCA service loses state (restart): docker compose restart rca-service. active_alerts dict is reset — any stale active alerts will be re-evaluated on next Prometheus scrape. Slack webhook URL set in docker-compose.yml SLACK_WEBHOOK environment variable. Zammad admin token in RCA service environment.
